title: Definitions of AI, machine learning, and generative AI
description: Define AI, machine learning, and generative AI, and understand how generative AI creates new content from learned patterns.

title: Common AI use cases in IT support
description: Learn how AI helps IT support with ticket triage, summarization, routing, proactive alerts, and faster responses.

title: AI-assisted search, summarization, and ticket triage
description: Use AI to summarize search results and support tickets, then route issues by urgency, intent, and expertise to speed resolution.

title: Risks: hallucinations, bias, privacy, and data leakage
description: Learn the main AI risks for A+ support work: hallucinations, bias, privacy exposure, and data leakage, plus practical validation habits.

title: Appropriate and inappropriate uses of AI tools in support workflows
description: Learn where AI helps in help desk workflows, where it creates risk, and how to use it responsibly in IT support.

title: Policy and governance considerations for end users and technicians
description: Learn how AI governance, accountability, and risk controls guide safe, ethical use of AI tools for end users and technicians.

title: Change management basics
description: Learn how change management controls IT updates with testing, approvals, risk analysis, rollback planning, and documentation.

title: Backup and restore concepts
description: Learn backup and restore fundamentals, including why backups matter, where copies are stored, and how restore planning supports recovery.

title: Asset tagging and chain of custody
description: Learn how asset tags and chain of custody support traceability, accountability, and secure handling of retired IT equipment.

title: Licensing and software compliance
description: Understand software license compliance, common license models, audit risks, and practical inventory and renewal controls for A+ support work.

title: Safety procedures and regulatory awareness
description: Learn safe work practices, ESD protection, electrical precautions, and key compliance concepts for A+ hardware and lab work.

title: Environmental and disposal considerations
description: Learn why e-waste needs responsible handling, how improper disposal harms the environment, and what compliance-focused disposal practices reduce risk.

title: Documentation quality and knowledge base usage
description: Learn how quality documentation and a knowledge base reduce repeat work, improve resolution speed, and support consistent IT service.

title: Communication with users and stakeholders
description: Learn how to communicate clearly with users and stakeholders, tailor updates, and keep incidents and projects aligned.

title: IT support roles, responsibilities, and escalation paths
description: Learn how help desk and tech support roles handle user issues, track tickets, and escalate complex problems to higher tiers.

title: Ticketing systems, documentation standards, and change tracking
description: Learn how ticketing systems centralize support, improve traceability, and support documentation and change tracking in IT service workflows.

title: Customer service, communication, and professionalism
description: Learn the customer service habits, communication skills, and professional behaviors that help you support users effectively in IT roles.

title: Remote support workflows and endpoint management basics
description: Learn how remote support tools streamline troubleshooting, secure access, and endpoint management for distributed IT teams.

title: Asset management, inventory, and lifecycle concepts
description: Learn how IT asset management tracks hardware and software, supports inventory accuracy, and follows assets through their lifecycle.

title: Safety, ESD, and environmental handling procedures
description: Learn practical safety, electrostatic discharge, and environmental handling habits for working inside computers and electronic equipment.

title: Basic troubleshooting methodology and root-cause thinking
description: Learn a structured troubleshooting approach that isolates symptoms, tests causes, and drives lasting fixes instead of quick guesses.

title: Motherboards, chipsets, BIOS/UEFI, and firmware settings
description: Learn how motherboard firmware initializes hardware, controls boot settings, and supports security features like TPM and Secure Boot.

title: CPUs, cooling systems, and thermal management
description: Learn how CPUs generate heat, how airflow and cooling hardware remove it, and why thermal paste and clear airflow matter.

title: Storage devices: HDD, SSD, NVMe, SATA, and M.2
description: Compare common storage devices and interfaces, including HDDs, SSDs, SATA, NVMe, and M.2, for A+ Core 1 exam readiness.

title: Power supplies, wattage, connectors, and protection features
description: Learn how PC power supplies convert AC to DC, how wattage and connectors affect compatibility, and why protection features matter.

title: Expansion cards and internal buses
description: Learn how expansion cards extend motherboard functionality and how internal buses move data between components.

title: PC cases, form factors, and airflow considerations
description: Learn how case size, motherboard form factor, and fan placement affect fit, cooling, and upgrade options in a PC build.

title: Input/output ports and connectors
description: Identify common display and peripheral ports, compare connector types, and choose the right cable for modern and legacy devices.

title: Peripheral devices and common accessories
description: Learn the common peripherals and accessories used with PCs and mobile devices, including printers, scanners, input devices, and shared-control hardware.

title: Laptop hardware components and replaceable parts
description: Learn the common laptop components, what is often replaceable, and why laptop repair depends on compact, manufacturer-specific design.

title: Docking stations, port replicators, and USB-C/Thunderbolt docks
description: Compare docking options, understand USB-C and Thunderbolt compatibility, and troubleshoot common dock power, video, and connection issues.

title: Mobile device types: smartphones, tablets, and wearables
description: Compare common mobile device types, their form factors, input methods, and typical uses for communication, media, and productivity.

title: Mobile device connectivity: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, NFC, hotspot, and tethering
description: Learn how mobile devices connect through Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, NFC, hotspot, and tethering, including common uses and practical limits.

title: Mobile device management basics
description: Learn how mobile device management enrolls devices, applies policies, and supports secure access to organizational resources.

title: Battery technologies, charging, and power optimization
description: Learn battery types, charging behavior, and practical power settings that help extend laptop and mobile device battery life.

title: Touchscreens, digitizers, cameras, microphones, and sensors
description: Learn how touch input, imaging, audio capture, and device sensors work together and how to troubleshoot common hardware and driver issues.

title: BYOD, COPE, and remote work support considerations
description: Compare personal and corporate device models, then apply practical support and security considerations for hybrid and remote work.

title: Printer types: laser, inkjet, thermal, impact, and multifunction devices
description: Compare common printer types, their output methods, and the practical features you need to recognize for A+ troubleshooting.

title: Printer installation, drivers, queues, and sharing
description: Install printers, choose the right drivers, manage print queues, and troubleshoot shared printer connections in Windows.

title: Common printer maintenance and consumables
description: Learn routine printer care, common consumables, and practical maintenance habits that help preserve print quality and reduce downtime.

title: Wireless and network printer setup
description: Configure wireless and network printers, verify addressing, and troubleshoot common connectivity issues in Windows and SOHO networks.

title: Print quality issues and troubleshooting
description: Diagnose common printer defects like streaks, smudges, banding, and faded output by checking consumables, rollers, fuser, drum, and paper.

title: 3D printing fundamentals
description: Learn the basic 3D printing workflow, common printer types, and core terms used to create models, slice files, print, and finish parts.

title: 3D printer components, filament/resin concepts, and basic workflow
description: Learn the main 3D printer parts, how filament and resin differ, and the basic additive manufacturing workflow from model to post-processing.

title: Common 3D printing failures and support considerations
description: Identify frequent 3D printing failures, trace likely causes, and apply practical support checks to improve first-layer success and print quality.

title: Safety and maintenance for print devices
description: Learn safe handling, routine upkeep, and basic maintenance habits that help print devices stay reliable and reduce avoidable failures.

title: Network types: LAN, WAN, WLAN, PAN, and MAN
description: Compare common network types by scope, speed, and typical use so you can identify LAN, WAN, WLAN, PAN, and MAN on A+ exams.

title: Network devices: routers, switches, access points, firewalls, and modems
description: Learn the roles of common network devices and how routers, switches, access points, firewalls, and modems support connectivity and traffic flow.

title: IP addressing, subnet basics, DHCP, DNS, and NAT
description: Learn core IPv4 and IPv6 addressing, subnet masks, DHCP, DNS, default gateways, and NAT for basic network connectivity.

title: Common ports and protocols
description: Learn the well-known TCP and UDP ports used by common services, and how port numbers help clients, servers, and firewalls communicate.

title: Wi-Fi standards, bands, channels, and security modes
description: Learn how 802.11 standards, frequency bands, channels, and security modes affect Wi-Fi compatibility, range, and protection.

title: Ethernet cabling, connectors, and standards
description: Identify common Ethernet cable types, connector styles, and twisted-pair standards used in A+ networking tasks.

title: VPN concepts and remote access basics
description: Learn how remote access VPNs create encrypted tunnels, support telework, and what to check when troubleshooting connectivity.

title: Internet connectivity troubleshooting
description: Diagnose internet connectivity issues by checking physical links, local IP settings, DNS resolution, and the troubleshooting sequence.

title: Network troubleshooting tools and commands
description: Practical Windows networking commands and troubleshooting checks for diagnosing connectivity, routing, and name-resolution issues.

title: Virtual machines and hypervisors
description: Learn how virtualization uses hypervisors to run isolated virtual machines and why type 1 and type 2 designs matter.

title: Desktop virtualization concepts
description: Learn how desktop virtualization and VDI centralize desktops, support remote access, and simplify management across many devices.

title: Cloud storage and synchronization
description: Learn how cloud storage syncs files across devices, how OneDrive and SharePoint work, and how to troubleshoot common sync issues.

title: SaaS, PaaS, and IaaS at a conceptual level
description: Compare the three core cloud service models by control, responsibility, and how much the provider manages for you.

title: Collaboration and productivity platforms
description: Learn how collaboration platforms support file sharing, co-authoring, online meetings, and teamwork across devices and locations.

title: Remote desktop and remote access tools
description: Learn how Remote Desktop and Remote Assistance work, what they require, and how to troubleshoot basic connection issues.

title: Shared resources and identity-based access concepts
description: Learn how identity, authentication, authorization, and identity-based access control protect shared resources across users, devices, and services.

title: Windows installation types, editions, and upgrade paths
description: Compare Windows installation methods, edition changes, and supported upgrade paths for Windows 10 and Windows 11 deployments.

title: Windows 11 hardware requirements and compatibility concepts
description: Learn the minimum Windows 11 hardware requirements, why TPM 2.0 matters, and how compatibility affects upgrade decisions.

title: Installation media, recovery media, and deployment basics
description: Learn how installation media, recovery media, and deployment methods support Windows setup, repair, and repeatable system installs.

title: User profiles, accounts, permissions, and UAC
description: Learn how Windows user profiles, account types, permissions, and User Account Control protect access and reduce unauthorized changes.

title: File systems and storage management
description: Learn how Windows file systems organize data, what NTFS, exFAT, FAT32, and UDF support, and how Disk Management handles partitions and volumes.

title: Device Manager, Disk Management, Task Manager, and Services
description: Learn how Windows admin tools help you inspect hardware, manage storage, monitor performance, and control background services.

title: System utilities and administrative tools
description: Learn the Windows utilities and administrative tools used to inspect system details, troubleshoot issues, and manage core components.

title: macOS and Linux support basics
description: Core support concepts for macOS and Linux, including startup troubleshooting, terminal use, and safe operating system installation.

title: Command-line fundamentals for support tasks
description: Learn how Windows command-line shells support troubleshooting, automation, navigation, and basic file operations in help desk workflows.

title: Mobile and remote-work security practices
description: Secure remote and mobile users with VPN, MFA, endpoint protection, BYOD policy, encryption, and safe public Wi-Fi habits.

title: Basic incident response awareness
description: Learn the purpose of incident response, how incidents differ from events and alerts, and the core phases of a response plan.

title: Security principles: CIA triad, least privilege, and defense in depth
description: Learn the core security principles of confidentiality, integrity, availability, least privilege, and layered protection for A+ exam readiness.

title: Authentication, authorization, and accounting
description: Learn how AAA controls access, enforces policy, and tracks user activity in the order authentication, authorization, then accounting.

title: Malware types and prevention
description: Learn common malware types, how they spread, and practical prevention steps to reduce infection risk on A+ systems.

title: Endpoint protection and antivirus
description: Learn how endpoint protection and antivirus tools detect, prevent, investigate, and respond to threats across modern devices.

title: Encryption concepts and secure storage
description: Learn how encryption protects data at rest, how BitLocker uses TPM, and why hardware-backed storage improves security and performance.

title: Physical security controls
description: Learn how physical security controls protect IT assets from unauthorized access, damage, theft, and disruption to confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

title: Social engineering and phishing
description: Learn how social engineering and phishing target people, use urgency and deception, and why awareness training helps reduce risk.

title: Secure browsing, email safety, and user awareness
description: Learn how browser protections, email hygiene, and user awareness reduce phishing, malware, and risky online behavior.

title: Update failures and patch-related issues
description: Troubleshoot Windows Update failures by checking prerequisites, running built-in diagnostics, and using rollback or recovery when needed.

title: Application installation and removal
description: Learn common Windows app removal paths, repair options, and troubleshooting steps when installs or uninstalls fail.

title: Compatibility issues and version conflicts
description: Learn why older apps and mismatched software versions fail after updates, and how to troubleshoot compatibility problems safely.

title: Browser troubleshooting
description: Diagnose common browser problems by updating, clearing cache and cookies, checking settings, and isolating extensions or compatibility issues.

title: Email and calendaring issues
description: Troubleshoot common Outlook email and calendar problems, including sync failures, invite handling, and support paths.

title: Productivity suite support
description: Support Microsoft 365 productivity apps by understanding app access, cloud features, and common repair and troubleshooting paths.

title: Printing from applications
description: Learn how Windows apps register for printing, use the print flow, and handle preview and dialog behavior.

title: File access, permissions, and association issues
description: Troubleshoot Windows file access, permission, and file association problems by checking access rights, privacy settings, and default apps.

title: Performance problems and resource contention
description: Identify common resource bottlenecks that slow systems, including CPU, memory, storage, network, and lock contention.

title: Startup and login issues
description: Troubleshoot Windows startup and sign-in problems by using Startup Settings, Safe Mode, and account recovery steps.
